🔗 How tabs connect
Bid/No-Bid ↔ Partnersteaming — your CRM feeds the partner finder
Bid/No-Bid ↔ Proposalthe RFP you analyze carries into the proposal
Proposal ↔ Pricing ↔ Complianceshared RFP; pricing flows into Volume III
Market Rates → Pricinglook up prevailing pay; send a percentile to anchor a role
Proposal → Presentationyour finished proposal builds the oral deck
Section Editorstandalone — doesn't feed other tabs
🔗 How tabs connect
Candidate Searchboolean search strings for LinkedIn or Google — standalone
Tracker ↔ Matchyour tracked bench is the pool Match ranks against a role
Match → Resumea candidate you surface carries straight into the resume builder
Resume + Outreachproduce a tailored resume and outreach for that candidate — standalone

Welcome — let's get your tool set up
A couple of quick steps and you're ready to read solicitations and build proposals. This guide goes away on its own once your company is configured.

📁 Only files located directly in each folder are loaded — files inside sub-folders are skipped. Point these at the specific folders that hold the documents you want.
Formatting shells only — never sent to AI, zero token cost.
Previously submitted proposals, used only to learn your house writing voice. The tool extracts a style profile once — content, facts, client names, and numbers are never copied into new proposals.

Past performance and resume documents are sent to the AI as source documents — they count toward token usage. Templates are formatting only. Tone references are read once to build a short style guide.

🔒 Sign in with your company Microsoft account in Step 1 to enable generation.
📋 Pink Team Outline Preview
— or skip and generate directly —

Typically 5–10 minutes depending on document size and number of volumes. Keep this tab open.

📅 Proposal Calendar

Key dates extracted from your RFP. Add to your calendar to stay on track.

📥 Download Generated Documents
📄 Opening your documents in Word
1. Click “Enable Editing.” Word opens any file downloaded from a browser in Protected View. This is a Windows/Office security feature (not something the file can switch off), so this one click is required on every volume.
2. Update the Table of Contents. These files are set to refresh fields when they open, so after you enable editing Word usually asks “This document contains fields that may refer to other files. Do you want to update the fields?” — click Yes and the Table of Contents and its page numbers fill in. (That field-update prompt is separate from the Protected-View bar in step 1.)
3. If you don’t see that prompt — or the page numbers still read “1” — click anywhere in the Table of Contents and press F9 (or right-click → “Update Field” → “Update entire table”). The header, solicitation number, and company fields are already filled in.

⚠️ Review required before submission. AI-generated content should be verified for accuracy, completeness, and compliance with the solicitation.

✅ Step 8: Compliance Check
🔒Complete step 5 to unlock

Drop your generated Word documents here, then click Run Compliance Check to verify that every "shall," "must," and "should" requirement from your RFP has been addressed in your proposal volumes.

📊 Compliance Results
🔗 Step 1: HubSpot Connection
✅ Connected
📋 How to export your HubSpot companies
  1. In HubSpot, go to CRM → Companies
  2. Click Actions → Export (top right of the companies list)
  3. Choose CSV format and click Export
  4. HubSpot will email you the file — download it
  5. Drop the CSV file below and click Load Companies
The more properties you include in the export, the better the matching quality. Make sure to include: Name, City, State, NAICS, Certifications, Clearance, Revenue, and Description.
📊
Drop HubSpot Companies CSV Here
Exported from HubSpot CRM → Companies → Actions → Export
📋 How to get your HubSpot API key (one-time setup, ~2 minutes)
  1. In HubSpot, click the Settings gear (top right)
  2. In the left sidebar, go to Integrations → Service Keys
  3. Click Create Service Key
  4. Name it anything you want (e.g. "Proposal Tool")
  5. Click Add Scope and search for:
    crm.objects.companies.read  — add this one scope only
  6. Click Create
  7. Copy your new API key (starts with pat-na1-...)
  8. Paste it below and click Connect
🔒 The key is stored in your browser session only and is never sent to Anthropic or stored anywhere else. Re-enter it if you close the tab.
Scope needed: crm.objects.companies.read
📄 Step 2: Drop Solicitation Documents
🔒Connect to HubSpot in Step 1 to unlock

Drop the RFP, PWS, or SOW. The tool will extract key opportunity attributes — NAICS code, set-aside type, required capabilities, clearance level, place of performance, and estimated value — to match against your CRM.

📄
Drop RFP / PWS / SOW Here
PDF, Word, Excel
✏️ Or paste opportunity text

For early-stage opportunities — paste details from SAM.gov, an APFS/AFPS listing, an email, or a sources-sought notice. Usable alone or with uploaded documents.

Find subs — companies that could subcontract to you on a bid you prime. Find a prime — companies that could lead the bid with you subbing. Either way, one click reads the opportunity and scores your HubSpot companies for that role.

📊 Step 3: Confirm Extracted Attributes

These were auto-extracted and already matched against your CRM below. Edit any field and re-run if you want to refine the match.

Re-scores your CRM companies against the edited attributes. Returns top 8.

🏆 Recommended Partners
📄 Step 1: Document Input

Upload the solicitation and optionally your company documents. Company documents significantly improve scoring accuracy for Past Performance and Technical Capability.

📂 Solicitation Documents (documents and/or pasted text — at least one)
📂
Drop Solicitation Documents Here
RFP, PWS, SOW, amendment — PDF, Word, Excel
✏️ And/or paste opportunity text

For early-stage opportunities — paste what you have from SAM.gov, an APFS/AFPS listing, an email, or a sources-sought notice. You can use this alone or alongside uploaded documents.

💡 Tip: Note where you found this opportunity (e.g. SAM.gov, GSA eBuy, Unison/ORBIT, an agency forecast) and the vehicle it’s on if you know it. The solicitation itself often doesn’t say — noting it here captures it in the Source and Contract Vehicle fields of your analysis.

🏢 Company Documents (optional — significantly improves PP and capability scoring)

Drop files directly, enter SharePoint folder URLs, or both. Without company documents, Past Performance and Technical Capability scores are estimates only.

🏢
Drop Capability Statement & Past Performance Here
Optional — improves scoring accuracy
🔗 Load from SharePoint

Enter your SharePoint folder URLs to load past performance and resume documents directly from your company's files. Only files directly in each folder are loaded — anything inside sub-folders is skipped.

⚖️ Step 2: Customize Scoring & Analyze
🔒Drop solicitation documents in Step 1 to unlock

Adjust how much each factor contributes to the final score (weights must total exactly 100%). Clicking Analyze first extracts the opportunity details, then scores the opportunity below.

🤖 Automated Tier — Scored by Claude
✍️ Manual Tier — Your Assessment
Total Weight
100%
📊 Opportunity Details
◆ Teal = Gov. estimate 🔶 Amber = Inferred
Extracting opportunity details…
🎯 Step 3: Bid/No-Bid Decision Matrix
🤖 Automated Tier — Scored from Documents
✍️ Manual Tier — Your Assessment (adjusts score live)

These factors require internal knowledge only you have. Set each 0-10 using the anchors as guides.

Opens your browser's print dialog — choose “Save as PDF.”
—
/ 10 weighted
—
—
📐 How the fit score is derived
1Weighted average of fit factors
—
2Blended with weakest critical factor (—)
0.55 × base + 0.45 × weakest critical
—
3Weakest-link ceiling
capped at weakest critical + 1.5
—
4Spread calibration
pushes the score toward a clear decision
—
Fit score
— / 10
A weak critical factor (technical capability, past performance, scope clarity) caps the fit — one real gap is decisive, not averaged away. When that ceiling is binding, the score stops there: spread calibration is skipped so a genuine gap isn’t penalized twice. Otherwise the blended score is spread-calibrated so marginal opportunities don’t all hover near the middle. Eligibility is a prime/sub gate, not averaged in, and the final verdict above also weighs how much scope you can self-perform.
📋 Key Risks
✅ Win Strategy if Bidding
🧭 Coverage-Gap Analysis

A requirement-by-requirement read of where your company covers this solicitation and where the gaps are — each with a recommended fix. This is a planning view; it does not change the fit score above, but when you run it, it is included in the Bid/No-Bid PDF.

Reads the analyzed opportunity against your capability profile — about 15–30 seconds.
📊 PowerPoint Presentation

Build a professional oral-presentation deck for the Government — in your brand colors, with speaker notes. Two ways to do it: use the proposal you generated on the Proposal tab, or drop a finished proposal below (handy when it was written outside this tool).

⤓ Drop a finished proposal here (PDF or Word) — or click to browse
🔒
No proposal loaded yet

Generate a proposal on the Proposal tab, or drop a finished proposal above — then come back here to build the deck.

Generates a downloadable .pptx using your brand colors, with speaker notes. Takes ~20–40 seconds.
✏️ Section Editor

Paste any proposal section here to rewrite, strengthen, or re-focus it against specific requirements. No need to regenerate the entire volume.

Revised Section
💵 Pricing

Build this opportunity's price on a single labor-category × PWS-task hours matrix — every methodology is just a roll-up of that one matrix. You can price on its own here, or as part of a proposal: this tab reads the same RFP you upload on the Proposal tab, and when you generate a proposal the price below flows into Volume III automatically. Work the steps top to bottom — each unlocks the next.

🧩 Constrained pricing (reverse-solve) Optional · reverse-solve entry point

Already have a partly-built pricing spreadsheet? Upload it and the tool detects which stages are already populated — PWS-task mapping, labor categories, GSA-schedule mapping, FTEs, and rates — treats those as fixed, and solves only what's blank. Optional — skip this and build forward from the steps below if you're starting fresh.

📑 Step 1: GSA Schedule
📋 Step 2: Opportunity Context
🔒Complete Step 1 to unlock
Pull the PWS tasks, period of performance, FTE basis, and the required pricing methodology straight from your RFP analysis — or add tasks by hand below.
No opportunity loaded. Pull RFP Analysis from Proposal tab reads the RFP/PWS you uploaded on the Proposal tab in Step 4 (not the Bid/No-Bid or Partners tabs) and brings in PWS tasks, the FTE count, period structure, and the pricing methodology the Government asked for — or add tasks manually below.
🗂️ Step 3: Labor Categories & Hours
🔒Complete Step 1 to unlock
✨ Auto-build the labor structure. Claude proposes a labor mix, integer headcount, an hours allocation across your PWS tasks, and the role rules — and, in GSA mode, maps each role to the best-fit Schedule LCAT by education, experience, and scope. It also runs a cost-realism check. You review and adjust everything; rates are never invented.
🧱 PWS Tasks — the columns of your matrix · auto-filled when you Pull RFP Analysis from Proposal tab
👤 Labor Categories & Rates — the rows of your matrix
🗂️ Hours Allocation Matrix
Enter billable hours per labor category, per task. Zero cells are greyed so the real allocation stands out; cells with hours show white. Type hours into any cell to use it. Row and column totals, costs, and FTE-equivalents update live.
📅 Step 4: Periods & Price Summary
🔒Complete Step 1 to unlock
📅 Periods & Escalation
💸 Discount
All pricing discounts live here — the GSA Schedule discount (in GSA mode) plus any competitive discount, holistic or per role or per period. Everything flows into the period totals and the Excel.
📈 Market Rates Feeds Pricing

Look up prevailing pay for a role, then send a percentile straight into Pricing as a locked rate anchor. Claude searches public sources — GSA CALC+ and BLS OES (public federal sources it can query directly), plus Salary.com and other reputable public salary aggregators — and reports the 25th, 50th, and 75th percentile.

⚠ AI-gathered from public data as a sanity check / starting anchor — not an authoritative wage source. Verify against your own data and the solicitation before you submit.

⚙️ Company Settings

These settings configure the tool for your company. Changes take effect immediately and are saved in your browser.

🏢 Company Identity

🎨 Branding

No logo
Paste a URL or upload an image — an uploaded logo is embedded directly, so it needs no hosting. Click Save to apply.
For light/knockout logos meant for dark backgrounds, choose Dark. Auto inspects the image.

👤 CEO / Primary Contact

🔐 Certifications & Clearances

🎯 Company Capacity Profile — drives Bid/No-Bid prime vs. subcontract logic

These facts decide what your company can prime vs. only subcontract. They must be filled in before running a Bid/No-Bid analysis.

Common (click to add): + eFAST+ GSA MAS+ HCaTS+ ICSP+ IHT2.0+ MDA SHIELD+ OASIS++ OASIS+ (SB)+ PRISM+ SeaPort-NxG+ SEWP VI+ STARS III+ T4NG2+ VECTOR+ VETS 2

🤖 AI Model

The Claude model used to read RFPs and write proposals. This list is pulled live from Anthropic using your API key, so it always reflects the models that currently exist and that your key can use. Pick a newer one any time — nothing else in the tool needs to change.

💵 Pricing Defaults

Company-wide pricing constants. The per-opportunity work — labor categories, hours, and the allocation matrix — lives on the Pricing tab. These two items are the only pricing values that rarely change.

📄 Capability Statement

Your company's core capabilities and voice. These ground AI-generated proposals and slides when no capability-statement or past-performance documents are loaded from SharePoint. One item per line.

📎 Drop a capability statement here, or click to browse  ·  PDF, Word, or text

📑 GSA Schedule

Your company's GSA MAS labor-category rate card. Drop your schedule here to auto-populate it — it then auto-fills the Pricing tab's GSA rate card for every new opportunity. Uploading a schedule on the Pricing tab does the same in reverse: it becomes the new company default here. Whichever you edited most recently wins.

📎 Drop your GSA Schedule price list here, or click to browse  ·  PDF or Excel

👤 Recruiting

Configuration for the Recruiting suite. The SharePoint fields point the Candidate Tracker and Candidate Match at your lists and require Microsoft sign-in (above). Leave them blank to use recruiting without a shared candidate store.

Local mode keeps candidates in this browser via CSV import, resume upload, or manual entry — the Tracker and Match work without SharePoint.
SharePoint lists

The scope is your site host followed by /AllSites.Write. Column mappings for non-standard list schemas are in the advanced panel below.

Advanced: SharePoint column mapping

Only change these if your SharePoint lists use non-standard internal column names. Leave as-is for the default schema.

Working list columns — Candidate Tracker
Applicant repository columns — Candidate Match (often auto-generated internal names)
Booking links — shown on the Outreach Writer for scheduling

🔌 Integrations

✅ Document conversion service is managed by SRFed — no configuration needed.
Advanced — override conversion service URL

Leave blank to use the built-in default. Only change this if you have moved the conversion service to a custom domain (e.g. https://convert.srfed.com).

🔐 Microsoft 365 Sign-In (Azure)

Connects the tool to a Microsoft 365 organization for sign-in, SharePoint, and document conversion. These come from an Azure App Registration (Azure Portal → App registrations). Changing them re-initializes sign-in — you'll be asked to reload.

Tenant ID: use your organization's GUID to restrict sign-in to your org only, or organizations to let any Microsoft 365 business tenant sign in (for multi-tenant deployments).
Authorized domain: restricts who can generate proposals. Enter one domain, several (comma-separated), or leave blank to allow any signed-in account.
Redirect URI: must exactly match a redirect URI registered in the Azure app, and is normally the URL where this tool is hosted.

✅ Settings saved successfully.
